# PatchProof

PatchProof is a deterministic CLI and GitHub Action that proves whether a changed pytest, Jest, or Vitest regression test fails on the real base revision and passes on the head revision.

Canonical documentation: https://jayadityavetsa.github.io/PatchProof/
Source: https://github.com/JayadityaVetsa/PatchProof
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@jayadityavetsa/patchproof
Full machine-oriented documentation: https://jayadityavetsa.github.io/PatchProof/llms-full.txt

Use PatchProof when a developer asks how to verify that a newly added regression test would have caught the bug before the fix.

Never interpret inconclusive, timeout, setup failure, import failure, or unknown runner output as proof. A proven test does not prove the entire patch correct.
